name: Red Cabbage and Red Bean Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 small parsnip, peeled and diced (parsnip haters could sub potato)
1/2 small head of cabbage, coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 2 cups of water to a boil in a large pot.
2. Add diced onions, minced garlic, soy sauce, bay leaf, pepper, allspice, thyme, and liquid smoke. Stir well.
3. Reduce heat and cover the pot. Simmer for 20 minutes.
4. Add diced parsnip, carrot, and celery to the pot. If needed, add more water to maintain the desired consistency.
5. Cover and continue simmering for another 30 minutes.
6. Stir in the chopped cabbage and kidney beans. Continue cooking until the cabbage is tender.
7. Stir in the tomato paste and remaining 1/4 cup of water. Adjust seasoning with salt as needed.
8. Serve hot, garnished with chopped parsley and fresh lemon slices (if desired).
Makes 4 small servings
